div.head: The Minister in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
818. Following telegram has been sent to American Consul General at Shanghai:
“September 16, 9 p.m. [Paraphrase.] Today Quo Tai-chiRepresentative of Wang Ching-wei. called to say that it was very likely Mr. Tong Shao-yiVeteran member of the Kuomintang. would approach you in order to transmit to the Legation a message for Wang Ching-wei. I pointed out, in reply, the possible impropriety if complying with such a request involved American officials in transmitting a message having a political character. It would appear advisable not to accept any message for transmission pending approval by the Department. Repeated to the Department. [End paraphrase.]”
